item.title: Oil prices sink, stocks soar after Trump announces deal with Iran
item.description: Markets rally on expectations that the agreement will ease global energy supply concerns, though analysts warn gas prices may remain elevated for some time.

item.title: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says Justice Department investigating him and his wife
item.description: California Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom said on Monday the Justice Department is investigating him and his wife. Newsom accused President Trump of using the DOJ to target him because of his political ambitions.

item.title: B-52 military jet crashes at Edwards Air Force Base in California
item.description: An Air Force B-52 crashed shortly after takeoff at California's Edwards Air Force Base on Monday, the base said. Emergency crews are responding.

item.title: Newsom says Justice Department is investigating him and his wife
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom, a Democrat, said the Justice Department is investigating he and his wife, Jennifer.

item.title: 3 CD account questions to ask after this week's Fed meeting
item.description: The Federal Reserve will meet again this week. Here are three CD account questions savers should ask afterwards.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: B-52 bomber crashes after takeoff at Edwards Air Force Base
item.description: Emergency crews responded to the scene, with the situation "ongoing," according to Edwards Air Force Base.

item.title: Knicks scored best point differential in NBA playoff history during championship run
item.description: The New York Knicks broke the record for highest point differential in playoff history during their run to the 2026 NBA Championship. Mike Vorkunov, national NBA and basketball business reporter for The Athletic, joins CBS News to discuss this team's place in NBA history.

item.title: Iran, Israel voice caveats on deal ahead of expected signing ceremony
item.description: Senior U.S. officials say President Trump and Iran's top negotiator have already remotely signed a memorandum of understanding ahead of an expected signing ceremony.

item.title: Challenges remain for U.S.-Iran deal as Israel battles Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's proxy war with Hezbollah in Lebanon continues while the U.S. looks to close a deal with Iran. While President Trump has said little about the exact terms of the U.S.-Iran deal, Iran says the deal would end hostilities on all fronts, including in Lebanon. CBS News senior foreign correspondent Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: What we know so far about the deadly skydiving plane crash in Missouri
item.description: A skydiving plane crash south of Kansas City, Missouri, killed 12 people, including the pilot, authorities said. CBS News' Lana Zak has more.

item.title: Trump in G7 summit in France as he touts signing of Iran memorandum
item.description: President Trump is in France for the annual G7 summit, as the world awaits a signing of a deal with Iran.

item.title: Why a cosmetics empire co-founder became a Catholic priest
item.description: Scott Vincent Borba was an accomplished business leader, making millions after he co-founded e.l.f. Cosmetics. Then he left everything behind to become a Catholic priest.

item.title: California daycare remains open after children test positive for drugs, investigation finds
item.description: About half a dozen parents told CBS California Investigates they had not been informed of the daycare's probationary status, even though the state requires the facility to inform all current and prospective parents.

item.title: How pet insurance can allow you to spend more on your pets
item.description: Pet insurance can do more than reduce your vet bills. It can also make room in the budget for other spending.

item.title: Latest on U.S.-Iran deal as Trump says text will be released "sometime after Friday"
item.description: President Trump said Monday that the terms of the U.S.-Iran deal will be released "sometime after Friday" as U.S. officials said the agreement had been signed electronically by both sides. CBS News' Aaron MacLean and Taurean Small have more.

item.title: How much interest will a $35,000 CD account earn now?
item.description: Interest earnings on a CD account of this size could be substantial and available to savers in just a few months.

item.title: Woman dies after being thrown from bridge without safety cord
item.description: In videos of the accident circulating online, two men launch the woman off Skeleton Bridge, while onlookers realize there is no safety mechanism attached.

item.title: Trump speaks separately with Zelenskyy, Putin as war rages on between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: President Trump spoke separately with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Russian President Vladimir Putin on Sunday, while the countries continue trading strikes. CBS News' Leigh Kiniry has more.

item.title: ICE agent allegedly struck by car in New Jersey
item.description: New Jersey officials are searching for a person who allegedly hit an ICE agent with their car. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.

item.title: Details emerge on Trump's stock trades
item.description: A CBS News investigation shows that President Trump made 3,600 stock trades across more than 1,000 firms within the first 3 months of his presidency. CBS News' Michael Kaplan has more.

item.title: Electricity costs expected to hit record this summer, new analysis finds
item.description: A new analysis projects how much Americans will pay for electricity from June to September, depending on their state of residence.

item.title: Hundreds of U.S. cities now have starter homes that cost $1 million
item.description: The number of metropolitan areas around the country with basic homes worth at least $1 million has tripled since 2020.

item.title: 3,600 stock trades in 3 months: Breaking down Trump's flurry of investments
item.description: President Trump's investment accounts traded between $212 million and $695 million in stocks and other securities in the first three months of the year — an unprecedented sum for a sitting president.

item.title: Trump made 3,600 stock trades in 3 months, data shows
item.description: A CBS News investigation found that President Trump made 3,600 stock trades in the first three months of 2026. CBS News' Michael Kaplan has more.

item.title: U.K. announces plan to ban social media for children under 16
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer hopes to have a ban on kids using social media that is "designed to be addictive" enacted by early next year.

item.title: Soccer art made from gum wrappers
item.description: Artist Lyndon J. Barrois Sr. painstakingly sculpts art depicting soccer players. His medium of choice? The humble chewing gum wrapper. Correspondent Luke Burbank talks with Barrois about his Los Angeles exhibit, "Fútbol Is Life: Animated Sportraits," which captures iconic moments from nearly 100 years of the World Cup in tiny pieces of paper and foil.

item.title: A key spy tool expired due to inaction in Congress. Here's what that means.
item.description: Lawmakers have long sounded the alarm about the risks of letting Section 702 expire. But there's debate over what a lapse in the law actually means.

item.title: Hilton to face Becerra in election for California governor, CBS News projects
item.description: Republican Steve Hilton, a former Fox News host and adviser to former British Prime Minister David Cameron, will advance to November's general election in the race to become California's next governor, CBS News projects.

item.title: GOP Rep. Kean returning to in-person work in "matter of weeks" after absence
item.description: The New Jersey congressman last voted on March 5.
